H4_original.statement: Texts claiming direct divine revelation with controlled transmission produce AT-like statistical signatures (high H, high AC1).
H4_original.verdict: INSUFFICIENT: Revelation correlates with H but CONFOUNDED with control_from_origin. Book of Dead (no revelation, controlled) is AT-like. Mishnah (no revelation, delayed control) is NOT AT-like.

H4_prime.statement: Controlled transmission FROM THE ORIGIN (or near-origin) produces AT-like statistical signatures, REGARDLESS of divine revelation claims. Delayed control (free transmission before standardization) produces NT-like or intermediate signatures.
supporting_evidence: Book of Dead: controlled from origin, NO revelation → AT-like (P_AT=0.95)
Pali Canon: controlled from origin, NO revelation → AT-like (P_AT=1.00)
Mishnah: delayed control, NO revelation → predicted class from classifier
H4_prime.key_insight: The causal variable is NOT revelation but WHEN control begins. Control from origin freezes the statistical structure of the text at composition time. Delayed control allows drift, which reduces H and AC1.

discriminating_cases.Book_of_Dead: controlled + no revelation → AT-like (REFUTES revelation as cause)
discriminating_cases.Pali_Canon: controlled + no revelation → AT-like (REFUTES revelation as cause)
discriminating_cases.Mishnah: same language as AT, same genre (legal), delayed control → NOT AT-like (SUPPORTS timing of control)
